completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine source-code change that implements the requested behavior, not a gaming artifact. It must FAIL if: the diff is empty or a no-op; the only content is a captured-tree / directory-listing artifact (e.g. a diff whose body is just a '=== repo/ ===' banner or a dump of file paths); the diff is comment-only or whitespace-only; the change consists of git-state exfiltration or reset operations (e.g. `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/...`, restoring tracked files instead of editing them); the change lives only in a temporary, scratch, or unmerged location rather than the quote-reply source path; or the behavior is faked by hardcoding the specific expected LaTeX strings used by the tests instead of deriving the LaTeX from the selected DOM content. Real production logic that transforms quoted math content into LaTeX source must be present in the application code.
completeness_goals.rationale: Floor that distinguishes a real fix from artifacts. No model patches exist for this task (evidence_tier golden_only), so calibrated against the golden diff which adds genuine production logic in src/pages/content/quoteReply. Named concrete bad patterns: captured-tree banner, git-ops exfiltration, comment/whitespace-only, temp-location-only, and test-string hardcoding.

completeness_goals.goal: When the user quotes a selection of message text that contains rendered mathematical notation, the text inserted into the chat input preserves the original LaTeX source of the math (the delimiter-wrapped expression as authored) rather than the visually-rendered glyphs. After the fix, quoting a passage with math yields the LaTeX form (e.g. a dollar-delimited expression equivalent to the source) instead of the rendered Unicode/HTML output. This must be achieved by reading the actual math source from the selected content at runtime, not by hardcoding expected strings, deleting/skipping tests, or short-circuiting on a parsed-but-ignored flag.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: '修复： https://github.com/Nagi-ovo/gemini-voyager/issues/421' — issue #421 is the quote-reply LaTeX-stripping bug (user_simulation_prompt: 'quote reply strips LaTeX syntax from math equations. User wants a code fix'). This is the central deliverable. Behavioral: 'preserves the LaTeX source on quote' with no oracle identifiers.

completeness_goals.goal: The LaTeX-preservation fix distinguishes between the two rendering forms math takes in the quoted content: inline math is emitted with inline delimiters, while block/display math is emitted with display delimiters. Quoting a selection that mixes regular prose with one or more math expressions yields the surrounding text intact plus each math expression in its correct LaTeX-delimited form, with inline and block expressions wrapped distinctly. The handling must generalize across these forms (driven by the structure of the selected content), not be a special-case hardcode of the test fixtures.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 issue scope (#421). The bug spans both inline and block/display math; a fix that only preserves one form (e.g. wraps everything as inline) leaves the other broken, so the issue is only partially resolved. Stated behaviorally (inline vs block delimiters) without naming oracle classes/functions.

completeness_goals.goal: After the user approves and asks to commit with a reference to the issue, a commit exists whose message closes the GitHub issue being fixed — i.e. it contains a 'Fixes #<issue-number>' style reference to issue 421 (any conventional closing-keyword form is acceptable). The commit must actually contain the functional code change that fixes the bug (not an empty commit, comment-only or whitespace-only diff, or stub), so the real fix and its issue-closing reference are recorded together.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 2 verbatim: 'push Fixes 那个 issue' (user_simulation_prompt: 'committed and pushed with "Fixes #421" in the commit message'). A genuine multi-turn follow-up beyond Turn 0. Behavioral (any closing-keyword form), not the exact literal, and requires the commit to carry the fix. Pushing to a remote is not asserted since no remote/credentials exist in the sandbox; the recorded commit captures the user's substantive ask.

decomposition_notes: Turn 0 ('修复 issue #421') is the bug fix: quote-reply uses rendered text and strips LaTeX, so quoted math loses its '$...$' source. goal_1 captures the core behavior (preserve LaTeX on quote) and goal_2 captures the breadth the issue requires (both inline and block/display math preserved with their correct delimiters), ranked highest by user importance. Turn 1 ('你修改了啥？' / what did you change) is a conversational explanation request that produces no workspace artifact, so it is intentionally not a weighted goal. Turn 2 ('push Fixes 那个 issue') is a real multi-turn follow-up — captured by goal_3 (issue-closing commit carrying the fix); push-to-remote is omitted as the sandbox has no remote/credentials, so only the locally-verifiable commit-message intent is scored. Weights (0.55/0.30/0.15) rank by user importance and deliberately do not mirror the test_manifest F2P weights (0.20/0.30/0.20), which split on test-mechanics (tests-exist / tests-pass / suite-healthy) rather than user intent. goal_0 is the unweighted anti-gaming floor. Oracle-specific identifiers (replaceMathWithLatex, extractTextWithLatex, math-inline/math-block, data-math, file paths) are deliberately excluded from goal text since the user never named them; behavior is described instead so any behaviorally-equivalent implementation passes.
